apex_basket.thesis: A 4x-FCF cash machine (~$20B TTM FCF) priced for terminal decline; the CRO held an 85% MoS after normalizing the rolling-over run-rate, and the $85B 'net debt > mkt-cap' is a lease/relative artifact the funded test (1.9x, 4.3x coverage) clears as solvent.
apex_basket.growth_durability: Flat revenue (3y CAGR +0.6%) and a declining broadband base, but a durable high-quality FCF engine (Piotroski 8/9), a credible self-help inflection (first YoY broadband improvement since 2020) and Epic Universe as a clean growth leg.
exposure_axes: theme-park / discretionary consumer spend (Epic Universe, parks ~7% of rev)

apex_basket.thesis: USPI ambulatory crown jewel (~40% margins) bolted to a deleveraging, FCF-gushing hospital book at 5.8x FCF, with management arbitraging the discount via buybacks; the funded test (2.3x / 4.2x, moderate) plus dossier-confirmed 'no significant maturities until late 2027' makes the moderate leverage acceptable, not a veto.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING: 7/7 beat-and-raise, FY25 EBITDA +14%, USPI EBITDA +12%; the one real near-term risk is the 2026 ACA EAPTC step-down / 2028 reimbursement path, not solvency.
exposure_axes: US hospital reimbursement regime (ACA EAPTC 2026, Medicaid SDP / 2028 DPP path)

apex_basket.thesis: A net-cash, ~10%/yr-buyback retailer at ~9.7x FCF where the CRO cut the optical 147% raw MoS to a still-positive 49% after normalizing the peak-EPS year; solvency is pristine (net cash, 0.8x funded) and a non-issue.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STABLE: best-in-cohort 14-straight-quarter top line (3y CAGR +12.5%), but FY26 was the first EPS decline of the era and op margin compressed to ~8% on tariffs/EMEA - a re-rating bet on a stalled-but-fortress compounder.

apex_basket.thesis: German out-of-home/digital-ad group at ~5.7x FCF vs JCDecaux ~15x, a 43.7%-margin OOH franchise bundled inside a conglomerate; the book's MOST-LEVERED apex name (3.0x / 3.7x, moderate), kept because its dossier shows NO near-term maturity wall - the value-unlock brake is founder-KGaA governance, not refinancing. Held at a REDUCED (~half) weight under the advertising-cycle combined cap because CMCSA already carries the larger ad leg.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STABLE tilting pivoting: OOH is a real franchise (adj EBITDA +12%, 43.7% margin) but DaaS/Statista is shrinking (-13.5% organic) and EPS has gone sideways-to-down; real FCF (EUR341m, 15.2% margin) funds the dividend+buyback even as leverage drifts up - leverage noted, not a veto.

apex_basket.thesis: A 30-year founder-led payments compounder mid-transformation from an 'ATM company' to a digital-payments-infrastructure SoP, near 52-week lows with an activist + first-Investor-Day-in-a-decade; the settlement/float balance sheet scores low on Altman-Z but the funded test (0.8x / 6.7x, strong) confirms ~1x leverage.
apex_basket.growth_durability: PIVOTING: organic revenue decelerated to +6% and GAAP FCF is shrinking (the per-share 'double-digit' is partly buyback-/convert-funded), offset by fast-growing higher-margin digital, Dandelion, CoreCard and acquiring legs.
exposure_axes: cross-border / migration transaction volume (remittance, US immigration & remittance-tax policy)

apex_basket.thesis: The single highest-quality name in the book - a near-monopoly, asset-light OTA (ROIC 32.7%, FCF margin 33.8%) at a depressed ~15x FCF on a transient Middle-East demand shock, buying back a record $3.6B/quarter into the dip; net-cash fortress (0.3x funded) makes solvency irrelevant.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STABLE: rev 3y CAGR +16.3%, adj-EPS compounding +20%+; the cracks are GAAP NI down ~8% vs adjusted, FX-flattered reported growth, and an AI-disintermediation terminal risk. The CRO's 33% MoS is conservative for this quality.

apex_basket.thesis: A PEO whose broken insurance book has been repriced ahead of schedule (ICR 90.8% -> 84%), driving +25% adj-EPS, at 7.3x FCF with a 9/9 Piotroski and a ~18%-of-cap buyback into the trough; the float-distorted Altman-Z is meaningless - the funded test (1.6x / 5.0x, strong, ~net cash) confirms a cash-gushing balance sheet.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING on the controllable half (margin/cost/capital-return delivered) but revenue is still contracting (-5% YoY Q1-26) with no dated path back to growth - the margin catalyst has largely fired, leaving a cheap-FCF wait on the top-line inflection.

apex_basket.thesis: A completed, credibly-executed reinsurance balance-sheet turnaround the reported financials still understate, at ~5x FCF and ~37% below its own economic value, riding a positive ratings cycle; the Altman-Z is a policyholder-reserve artifact and the funded view is net cash with 220% solvency - the textbook 'cleared for the right reason' name.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING: record FY25 net income EUR846m / 19.1% ROE, reserve buffers built two years early, ratchet-protected rising dividend. P&C margins sit cyclically near peak into a softening 2026 reinsurance market.

apex_basket.thesis: A net-cash specialty-ingredient compounder whose multi-year margin-recovery streak is interrupted (not broken) by the self-inflicted Argo plant accident; solvency is a fortress (net cash, 0.8x funded, 25.5x coverage) and the CRO held a 40% MoS after normalizing the Argo-hit, guidance-cut quarter.
apex_basket.growth_durability: STABLE tilting deteriorating near-term: revenue in secular slow-shrink (3y CAGR -3.1%, all price/mix) and FY26 EPS guided flat-to-down, but the Texture & Healthful Solutions franchise (GLP-1 protein tailwind) is genuinely secular.

apex_basket.thesis: DIVERSIFICATION SWAP for the hospital-reimbursement cluster: a near-duopoly tax-prep franchise at 8.7x FCF, STRENGTHENING (Assisted share stabilized FY26 Q3, Direct File killed = a regulatory TAILWIND, OBBBA complexity tailwind), strong funded solvency (1.1x / 12.7x); CHOSEN because its exposure axes are orthogonal - counter-cyclical tax-prep, not hospital reimbursement, advertising, travel or reinsurance - and it does not re-cluster (ARDT re-clusters with hospitals, so was rejected).
apex_basket.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING: 71% beat rate, reaffirm-then-raise across 8 quarters, ~6% of shares retired in FY26; the open bear debate is slow AI-erosion of paid prep, the bull is a cheap counter-cyclical compounder re-rating as the AI bogeyman fades.

runner_ups.thesis: DIVERSIFIED OUT of the apex for correlation, NOT for value: a high-quality acute+behavioral operator at single-digit forward P/E with 48% CRO MoS and strong funded solvency (1.8x / 13.4x) - apex-grade on standalone value, but it shares THC's single 2028 US-hospital-reimbursement / Medicaid-DPP factor, so per the hidden-factor stress it is benched and THC (higher value: 82% vs 48%, credibility 5 vs 4) carries the hospital leg alone.
runner_ups.growth_durability: STABLE operationally but with a dated structural headwind: 2024-25 EPS was Medicaid-directed-payment-funded, scheduled to be reduced from 2028 (UHS's own ~$432-480M/yr revenue-reduction estimate by 2032). The 48% sop_mos is the CRO splitting that bull/bear gap.
exposure_axes: US hospital reimbursement regime (Medicaid DPP 2028, work-requirements 2027)

runner_ups.thesis: Gartner is cheap-vs-peers with a strong funded balance sheet (1.3x / 15.6x), but forensic_gate=CAP (DETERIORATING) holds the score at the ~50 ceiling despite a 43% sop_mos; peak_flag true so the headline multiple needs the normalized view.
runner_ups.growth_durability: DETERIORATING trajectory per the interrogator - credible but worsening; capped, not excluded.

runner_ups.thesis: Greif (industrial packaging) is cheap_vs_peers and STRENGTHENING with strong funded solvency (1.8x / 31.5x); held below the apex because it is peak_flag AND stale, so the raw MoS is discounted to the CRO's still-positive 31%.
runner_ups.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING but cyclical/stale earnings require mid-cycle normalization; lost the Consumer-Cyclical seats to ANF, BKNG and the HRB diversification swap.

runner_ups.thesis: Simply Good Foods is a 38% sop_mos clean-label/protein name with acceptable moderate funded leverage (2.0x / 10.2x) and no maturity-wall concern; in_line on peers and freshness-stale, ranking just behind INGR for the Consumer-Defensive seat.
runner_ups.growth_durability: PIVOTING; leverage noted but not a veto (healthy coverage, no wall).

runner_ups.thesis: Swiss Re is a second 'cleared-for-the-right-reason' reinsurer (exempt_financial; low Altman-Z is a reserve artifact), cheap_vs_peers and STRENGTHENING; behind SCR.PA on MoS AND - critically for the stress - it RE-CLUSTERS with SCR.PA on the reinsurance pricing/cat cycle, so it cannot serve as a diversifying apex add.
runner_ups.growth_durability: STRENGTHENING; verdict-C but solvency is exempt and the MoS is positive - a credible insurer runner-up, kept off the apex to avoid a reinsurance double-leg with SCR.PA.
exposure_axes: reinsurance pricing / cat cycle (RE-CLUSTERS with SCR.PA)

runner_ups.thesis: Ardent Health is statistically cheap (33% sop_mos, 4.7x FCF, strong funded 1.2x / 11.2x) but a borderline value trap (ROIC 4.7% < WACC, EPS -39%, first down-EBITDA year guided, CEO departed 2026-06-02) AND it RE-CLUSTERS with the hospital-reimbursement factor (THC/UHS), so it was explicitly rejected as the diversification swap - 'cheap-for-a-reason,' not apex-grade.
runner_ups.growth_durability: PIVOTING with sub-WACC returns and fresh CEO-transition governance risk; a value-with-execution-risk runner-up that re-clusters with hospitals.
exposure_axes: US hospital reimbursement regime (RE-CLUSTERS with THC/UHS)

value_memo:
RUBRIC WEIGHTING. Four pillars ~25 pts each, applied only to names that clear the forensic gate: (1) Margin of Safety anchored on the CRO-reconciled sop_mos_pct (system of record), cross-checked against the 5-method mos_spread agreement but discounting any model MoS on a peak/stale name; (2) Cyclical-peak vs durable-growth normalization applied BEFORE crediting cheapness; (3) FUNDED-LEVERAGE SOLVENCY (net_funded_debt_ebitda + interest_coverage; raw Altman-Z ignored); (4) Multiples vs true peers + growth durability/quality. The catalyst-regime overlay is fully removed. Hard constraint: <=3 names per sector. NEW THIS RUN: a mandatory HIDDEN-FACTOR CORRELATION STRESS over the final 10 before sizing (the <=3/sector cap is NOT a correlation control).

FORENSIC GATE - EXCLUDED (interrogator credibility <=2; ineligible regardless of cheapness): AFX.DE, BRBR, CORT, HUBG, LIGHT.AS, MOH. CAPPED at ~50 (DETERIORATING but credible): BMW.DE, DMLP, GBX, GRBK, INSP, IT, JUN3.DE, KBH, NEXI.MI, ORI, PAH3.DE, PPC, SAM, TFI.PA, UVV. Among the capped, only IT (43% sop_mos, cheap, strong funded - top runner-up) and NEXI.MI (56% sop_mos but DETERIORATING and stale) screen as value-relevant; both sit in the capped pool, never the apex.

CYCLICAL-PEAK / STALE ARTIFACTS DOWN-RATED - BRBR and CALM EXPLICITLY. BRBR (BellRing) is the headline trap and is EXCLUDED outright (forensic credibility 2, funded_solvency=weak 4.2x / 3.5x, peak AND stale, DETERIORATING): its raw scan MoS was +335% on peak earnings, but the CRO's normalized fair value collapses it to a sop_mos of just +42% - and the forensic gate makes the point moot. CALM (Cal-Maine) is the cleanest peak artifact in the set: a raw scan MoS of +391% on an egg-windfall peak (freshness-stale, latest quarter rolling over) that the CRO normalized down to a sop_mos of just +8% - a low multiple on peak earnings is NOT value; CALM is excluded from both lists. Within the apex, the peak/stale normalization was applied and SURVIVED with a still-positive normalized MoS for CMCSA (raw 127% -> CRO 85%), ANF (raw 147% -> 49%), INGR (raw 44% -> 40%) and HRB (peak_flag normalized, CRO 23%); each is marked peak_normalized=true. THC, EEFT, BKNG, SCR.PA carry peak_flag=true but are durable-growth cases (not stale, positive revenue CAGR, healthy ROIC), so the high ratio was NOT normalized away.

VERDICT-C NAMES. No verdict-C name is seated in the apex (every apex name is verdict-B). SREN.SW (verdict-C reinsurer) is retained only as a runner-up on positive normalized MoS with exempt-financial solvency, behind SCR.PA - and is additionally disqualified from an apex slot because it RE-CLUSTERS with SCR.PA on the reinsurance cycle. ARDT (verdict-B but ROIC 4.7% < WACC, EPS -39%, first down-EBITDA-year guide, CEO departed 2026-06-02) is flagged trap_flag=true, held to the runner-ups, and additionally re-clusters with the hospital-reimbursement factor.

==================== HIDDEN-FACTOR CORRELATION STRESS (run over the final 10 BEFORE sizing) ====================
correlation_stress: The <=3/sector cap is explicitly NOT treated as a correlation control. I decomposed the 10 on the hidden factors GICS misses: (a) end-market demand cycle, (b) regulatory/reimbursement regime, (c) advertising cycle, (d) rate/credit sensitivity, (e) single shared macro (one commodity / one FX / one policy). Every factor carrying >=2 names is named and resolved below.

CLUSTER 1 - US HOSPITAL-REIMBURSEMENT REGIME (regulatory): THC + UHS  ==> RESOLVED BY DIVERSIFICATION (swap UHS -> HRB). This is the tightest and most dangerous cluster: a SINGLE binary outcome (the 2028 Medicaid Directed-Payment-Program phase-down / US hospital-reimbursement ruling, plus the 2026 ACA EAPTC step-down and 2027 work-requirements) would hit BOTH legs at once - exactly the 'one ruling, two full-size legs' the rule forbids. THC is materially the higher-value, higher-conviction leg (sop_mos 82.0 vs 48.1; value 84 vs 66; credibility 5/STRENGTHENING vs 4/STABLE; and UHS's 2028 cliff is its OWN binding risk). I therefore KEEP THC as the sole hospital-reimbursement leg and SWAP OUT the lower-value leg UHS. Replacement = the best ORTHOGONAL eligible runner-up that does NOT re-cluster: HRB (H&R Block). ARDT was rejected because it RE-CLUSTERS with hospitals; the chosen name HRB carries counter-cyclical tax-prep demand + a US-tax regulatory TAILWIND (Direct File cancellation, OBBBA complexity) + an AI-substitution debate - none of which touch hospital reimbursement, advertising, travel or reinsurance. HRB is fully eligible (clears the gate, credibility 4, STRENGTHENING, cheap_vs_peers, strong funded 1.1x / 12.7x, no peak issue). UHS drops to the top of the runner-ups, flagged 'diversified out for correlation, NOT for value.' Sector check after the swap: Consumer Cyclical now ANF+BKNG+HRB = 3 (at the cap, still legal); Healthcare drops to THC alone.

CLUSTER 2 - ADVERTISING CYCLE: CMCSA + SAX.DE  ==> RESOLVED BY KEEP-WITH-COMBINED-SIZE-CAP. CMCSA carries US cable/NBCU ad sales + theme-park (Epic Universe) discretionary spend; SAX.DE is a ~pure European out-of-home / DOOH advertiser. A broad ad-recession is a shared factor and is real. I keep BOTH rather than swap, because the exposure is ASYMMETRIC and geographically split: CMCSA's advertising/parks exposure is a MINORITY slice of a broadband-FCF machine (parks ~7% of revenue; NBCU linear ad is being de-emphasized/Versant-spun), whereas SAX.DE is ad-pure; and the geographies barely co-move (US cable + US theme-park spend vs German/European OOH budgets). The control is an EXPLICIT COMBINED-SIZE CAP: CMCSA + SAX.DE together are sized to carry no more than a single full-size advertising bet, implemented by holding SAX.DE at a REDUCED (~half) weight since CMCSA already supplies the larger, US-side ad/parks leg. Justification: a single ad-downturn cannot hit two equal full-size legs - one leg (SAX.DE) is deliberately half-sized, and the two legs are split across asymmetric intensity and across the US vs European ad markets. No hidden factor quietly carries two full-size legs.

CLUSTER 3 - DISCRETIONARY-CONSUMER DEMAND: ANF + BKNG  ==> RESOLVED BY KEEP-WITH-COMBINED-SIZE-CAP (+ a counter-cyclical offset). ANF (US teen apparel/goods) and BKNG (global travel/leisure services) both ride discretionary consumer demand. I keep both with a combined discretionary-demand size cap rather than swapping, because they sit in DIFFERENT sub-cycles (apparel GOODS, US/tariff/EMEA-exposed vs travel SERVICES, global, Middle-East-shock-exposed) and DIFFERENT geographies, so they do not co-move tightly, and both are net-cash fortresses (solvency orthogonal). Crucially, the THIRD Consumer-Cyclical seat - the HRB diversification add - is COUNTER-cyclical (tax-code complexity rises in downturns; OBBBA tailwind), so it OFFSETS rather than compounds the discretionary beta of the sleeve. Combined-size cap on ANF+BKNG = one-and-a-half discretionary units (each held modestly under full size); justified by the sub-cycle + geographic split and the counter-cyclical HRB offset.

OTHER >=2 CHECKS RUN (and cleared):
 - EEFT + TNET 'transaction-volume' (flagged candidate): DECOMPOSES TO A FALSE CLUSTER. EEFT's volume is CROSS-BORDER consumer remittance/travel money driven by migration & remittance-tax policy; TNET's 'volume' is DOMESTIC US SMB worksite-employee COUNT driven by small-business HIRING. Different real drivers (cross-border migration flows vs US SMB labor demand), low co-movement - so they are NOT a true >=2 cluster and both stay, un-capped on this axis.
 - ANF + BKNG (+ EEFT travel-money) consumer-discretionary: handled as Cluster 3; EEFT's cross-border/remittance demand is a distinct (migration-policy) driver, not the same discretionary-goods/travel-services beta, so it is noted but not folded into the cap.
 - RATE / CREDIT: SCR.PA (reinsurer, rate-sensitive) is the only true rate/credit name; CMCSA/THC carry heavy ABSOLUTE leverage but on the funded test are solvent and their credit exposure is idiosyncratic, not a shared macro - no >=2 rate cluster.
 - FX / single macro: EUR appears in SAX.DE and SCR.PA, but SAX.DE is capped on the ad axis and SCR.PA is a global reinsurer whose driver is the underwriting cycle, not EUR spot - EUR is a second-order shared factor, not a primary >=2 cluster, and is left noted.
NET OF THE STRESS: one DIVERSIFICATION (UHS -> HRB, removing the single-ruling hospital double-leg) and two KEEP-WITH-COMBINED-SIZE-CAPS (advertising CMCSA+SAX.DE with SAX.DE half-weighted; discretionary ANF+BKNG with a combined cap and an HRB counter-cyclical offset). After the stress, no single hidden factor (hospital ruling, ad-recession, discretionary-consumer slump, FX, rate, or commodity) carries two full-size apex legs.

==================== END CORRELATION STRESS ====================

FUNDED-LEVERAGE SOLVENCY (pillar 3). Cleared 'for the right reason' (low Altman-Z = reserve/float/lease artifact, not real funded debt): SCR.PA (exempt_financial, 0.4x, net cash, 220% solvency - textbook); EEFT (0.8x / 6.7x) and TNET (1.6x / 5.0x, ~net cash) clear on their settlement-/payroll-float books; CMCSA (1.9x / 4.3x) clears despite the $85B net-debt>mkt-cap flag (lease/relative, healthy coverage; net debt NOT credited as net cash); ANF, BKNG, INGR, HRB carry genuine net cash or ~1x funded. Real funded leverage kept with leverage noted (none jointly weak with a maturity wall): THC 2.33x / 4.2x (moderate; dossier-confirmed no maturities until late 2027) and SAX.DE 3.0x / 3.7x (moderate; the book's most-levered apex name, no near-term wall, founder-KGaA the real brake). UHS (1.8x / 13.4x, strong) was solvency-fine - it left the apex for correlation, not solvency.

RISE / FALL vs the PRIOR value apex [CMCSA, THC, ANF, SAX.DE, EEFT, UHS, BKNG, TNET, SCR.PA, INGR]:
- CMCSA: HELD (apex). Now carries an explicit advertising-cycle combined cap with SAX.DE; still the top pick on the 4x-FCF value.
- THC: HELD (apex). Becomes the SOLE hospital-reimbursement leg after UHS is diversified out; the higher-value, higher-conviction half of that pair.
- ANF: HELD (apex). Now sized under the discretionary-consumer combined cap with BKNG.
- SAX.DE: HELD (apex) but at a REDUCED (~half) weight under the advertising-cycle cap (CMCSA carries the larger ad leg).
- EEFT: HELD (apex). Cross-border/remittance volume confirmed distinct from TNET (no transaction-volume cluster).
- UHS: FALLS to runner-up - DIVERSIFIED OUT for correlation, NOT value. It is apex-grade on standalone value (48% MoS, strong funded), but shares THC's single 2028 hospital-reimbursement ruling, so the stress benches it and keeps THC alone on that factor.
- BKNG: HELD (apex). Sized under the discretionary-consumer combined cap with ANF.
- TNET: HELD (apex). SMB-employment factor confirmed orthogonal to EEFT.
- SCR.PA: HELD (apex). Sole reinsurance leg; SREN.SW kept OFF the apex precisely because it would re-cluster on the reinsurance cycle.
- INGR: HELD (apex). Defensive staple / ag-input axis; orthogonal to every other leg, no cap needed.
- NEW ADD - HRB: RISES into the apex as the DIVERSIFICATION SWAP for UHS - chosen as the best orthogonal, non-re-clustering runner-up (counter-cyclical tax-prep + a regulatory tailwind), which also de-betas the Consumer-Cyclical sleeve.
Net: the correlation stress made exactly ONE membership change (UHS -> HRB) to break the single-ruling hospital double-leg, and applied two combined-size caps (advertising CMCSA+SAX.DE; discretionary ANF+BKNG) so no hidden factor carries two full-size legs - a 10-name pure-value apex spanning 7 sectors, each <=3 per sector, each clearing the forensic gate with a still-positive CRO-normalized margin of safety.

stress_test.note: Market-based stress: weighted basket return to the 52-week lows, and to 52w-lows -15% (recession). cro_bear is the agents' own adverse SoP (bear_fv_px); when missing or implying upside it is flagged invalid and the published downside is the market-based recession stress.

correlation.fx_note: EU names in local ccy; correlations unadjusted for FX.

pool_stats.banner: Best-of-B basket: 0 verdict-A names in a 162-name pool — every apex pick is a verdict-B value name. Expect SLOW gap-closure: margin-of-safety re-rating, no hard catalysts by design.

funnel.note: scan MoS = membership/divergence signal only; never a rank or weight
